content.problemStatement:
Chef has met up with his old friend Jack recently. Jack told Chef that he found a recipe for an ancient dish, but unfortunately, it is locked in a safe and to open the safe, it is necessary to enter the solution to the following problem.

There is a sequence of non-negative integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč on the safe. Find the number of pairs (l,r)(l, r)(l,r) (1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N1 \leq l \leq r \leq N1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N) which satisfy the condition (Al‚ą®Al+1‚ą®‚Ä¶‚ą®Ar)‚äē(Al‚ąßAl+1‚ąß‚Ä¶‚ąßAr)‚Č•max‚Ā°(Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,Ar)‚ÄČ.(A_l \lor A_{l+1} \lor \ldots \lor A_r) \oplus (A_l \land A_{l+1} \land \ldots \land A_r) \geq \max(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \,.(Al‚Äč‚ą®Al+1‚Äč‚ą®‚Ä¶‚ą®Ar‚Äč)‚äē(Al‚Äč‚ąßAl+1‚Äč‚ąß‚Ä¶‚ąßAr‚Äč)‚Č•max(Al‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äč).

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR, ‚ą®\lor‚ą® denotes bitwise OR and ‚ąß\land‚ąß denotes bitwise AND.

Jack and Chef cannot think of anything except the recipe, so you are asked to open the safe.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** for each valid iii, AiA_iAi‚Äč is a power of 222
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of pairs (l,r)(l,r)(l,r) that satisfy the given condition.
